channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Marcos to decide on oil tax reduction bill this week
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. announced that he may finalize and sign a bill reducing excise taxes on oil products by Wednesday. The measure, backed by both chambers of Congress, aims to m...

item.title: Pakistan’s Mediation Offers Hope for De-escalation Between US and Iran
item.description: The editorial discusses a moment of respite in the tense US-Iran standoff after President Donald Trump decided to step back from his threat to bomb Iranian energy facilities unless Tehran reopened the...

item.title: Lawyer shot dead after speaking to car guard in Joburg CBD
item.description: Chinette Gallichan, a lawyer working on a case related to the unfair dismissal of miners, was tragically shot dead in the Joburg CBD. Before the attack, she exchanged a brief and friendly conversation...

item.title: Tanzania co-ops urged to adopt digital scales for transparency and proper payments
item.description: Tanzania Cooperative Development Commission (TCDC) imeongeza msukumo kwa ushirika za wakulima kutumia mizani ya kidijitali ili kupunguza udanganyifu, kuongeza uwajibikaji na kulinda maslahi ya wakulim...

item.title: Tanzania signs major niobium mining and processing agreement with US firm
item.description: Tanzania government don finalize agreements with Panda Hill Tanzania Limited, which na US-owned company, to speed up production and processing ya strategic minerals, especially niobium. The project ip...

item.title: Parliament Budget Session Update: Nirmala Sitharaman's Response to Finance Bill 2026 in Lok Sabha
item.description: The ongoing Budget session of Parliament on March 25, 2026, saw Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman responding to the discussion on the Finance Bill 2026 in the Lok Sabha. The bill is a crucial ...

item.title: Tanzania Invites Austrian Investors to Support Vision 2050 Economic Growth
item.description: Tanzania is actively seeking to attract more investment from Austria, with 17 Austrian-backed projects already registered in the country, valued at 36.14 million USD (about 92 billion Tanzanian Shilli...

item.title: Nationwide Transport Strike Set for Two Days Amid Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: Various transport and urban poor organizations across the Philippines are set to join a nationwide transport strike lasting two days in response to the recent surge in fuel prices. The move comes as l...

item.title: Naira Shows Slight Recovery Against Dollar on March 25, 2026
item.description: Today, March 25, 2026, the Nigerian Naira dey show small recovery as the week midpoint reach. For the official market, early trades show say the Naira don firm small, opening around ₦1,375.26 and late...

item.title: Zanzibar diversifies tourism offerings to attract international visitors
item.description: Zanzibar is expanding its tourism sector by introducing new products and increasing international visibility, moving beyond its traditional beach tourism focus. The government, under the leadership of...

item.title: Police Killings in Kenya Increased in 2025 Amid Protest Crackdowns, Report Shows
item.description: A recent report by the Missing Voices coalition has highlighted a worrying rise in police killings in Kenya during 2025. The report documented 131 cases of extrajudicial killings and enforced disappea...

item.title: ABC staff stage nationwide strike over pay dispute for first time in 20 years
item.description: ABC staff have walked off the job across Australia for the first time in two decades, protesting unresolved pay disputes. The strike, initially scheduled for 24 hours, has disrupted flagship programs ...

item.title: Kenya’s Growing Debt Controversy: A Look at Loan Legitimacy and Project Delays
item.description: In Kenya, public debt has once again become a key issue ahead of the 2027 elections, with constitutional lawyer Willis Evans Otieno challenging the legitimacy of some loans. He argues that certain bor...

item.title: Exploring beauty standards through Ruby's birthmark in 'The Aphorism Club'
item.description: In her latest novel, *The Aphorism Club*, Tracey Lee delves into the struggles of women in the early 20th century, using the protagonist Ruby Pearl Hart to explore how beauty standards have affected g...

item.title: UK government launches pilot to test social media restrictions for teenagers
item.description: The UK government has announced a six-week pilot programme to evaluate the effects of social media restrictions on young people. Organised by the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSI...

item.title: India’s Diplomatic and Economic Engagement with Iran Post-1979 Revolution
item.description: In the late 1970s, as political unrest intensified in Iran, Indian diplomats closely monitored the weakening authority of the Pahlavi regime. While India had maintained relations with Iran, the Shah's...

item.title: Philippine Airlines Secures Jet Fuel Supply Until June 2026 Despite Middle East Conflict
item.description: Philippine Airlines (PAL), led by Lucio Tan, announced that it has secured sufficient jet fuel to sustain its flight operations until June 2026. The airline clarified that no flights are being cancele...

item.title: Karachi's Weather Update: No Rain Expected Today, But Showers Likely Later This Week
item.description: Karachi was initially expected to receive drizzle and light rain today, but the Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has revised its forecast. PMD Deputy Director Anjum Nazeer confirmed that the s...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Urges End to Hostilities and Normalcy in Middle East During Call with Saudi Crown Prince
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif recently held a telephonic conversation with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed Bin Salman (MBS), urging the immediate cessation of hostilities and a return to normalcy amid ris...

item.title: Malian Journalist Sentenced to Two Years in Jail for Criticising Niger President
item.description: The Committee to Protect Journalists don beg Malian government make dem release journalist Youssouf Sissoko, wey dem sentence to two years jail plus fine of 1 million West African francs (around US$1,...
